text:
AUDIT shared layout primitives in an Expo Router app at '/Users/mikhail/Documents/CURSOR CODES/In Progress/backfire'. Scope: components/*.tsx (ScreenContent, LegalScrollScreen, WebAwareModal, ErrorBoundary, others), features/lobby/**, and all router layout files: app/_layout.tsx, app/(app)/_layout.tsx, app/(auth)/_layout.tsx, app/(app)/play/_layout.tsx, app/admin/_layout.tsx, app/(admin)/_layout.tsx. Questions: 1) What screen-scaffold components exist and which screens actually import them (grep usage counts)? 2) Do the router _layout files configure headers/safe-areas consistently (headerShown, screenOptions, contentStyle)? 3) Is there duplication between app/admin/ and app/(admin)/ routes — are they copies with divergent styles? 4) Are modals presented consistently (compare docs/modals.md to actual usage of WebAwareModal / presentation:'modal')? 5) Where do screens re-implement what a shared scaffold could provide (safe area + gutter + maxWidth + scroll)? Output: inventory of primitives with usage counts, gaps, and a recommendation for what a single shared Screen scaffold should own vs what stays per-screen. Read constants/theme.ts for tokens. Do NOT edit anything.

---
**Output:**
Write your findings to exactly this path: /Users/mikhail/Documents/CURSOR CODES/In Progress/backfire/.pi-subagents/artifacts/outputs/43f2c386/.pi-subagents/artifacts/outputs/audit-primitives.md
This path is authoritative for this run.
Ignore any other output filename or output path mentioned elsewhere, including output destinations in the base agent prompt, system prompt, or task instructions.

text:
[local file preview at transcript time; Cursor read result content was unavailable]
# Modals

_Learn how to use modals in Expo Router._

[Using Modals with Expo Router](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gNzuJVRmyDk)

Modals are a common user interface pattern in mobile apps. They are used to present content on top of the existing screen and is used for different purposes, such as displaying confirmation alerts or standalone forms. You can create modals in your app using the following methods:

- Use React Native's [`Modal`](https://reactnative.dev/docs/modal) component.
- Use Expo Router's special file-based syntax to create a modal screen within the app's navigation system.

Each approach has its specific use case. Understanding when to use each method is important for creating a positive user experience.

## React Native's Modal component

The `Modal` component is part of React Native's core API. Common use cases include:

- Standalone interactions, such as self-contained tasks that don't need to be part of the navigation system.
- Temporary alerts or confirmation dialogs that are ideal for quick interactions.

[358 more lines in file. Use offset=21 to continue.]

content.thinking:
BootScreen, ProfileAuthGate, and HeroSection appear to be dead code with no live imports outside test mocks.

A broader grep is verifying ProfileAuthGate and HeroSection. The audit document is being drafted. ScreenContent.tsx wraps five direct screens plus two legal screens via LegalScrollScreen and six play screens via PlayScaffold. Settings uses five WebAwareModal instances. Play/index handles navigation. Game-recap scaffolding and layout contentStyle are also under review.

ErrorBoundary covers root and admin layouts. PlayScaffold spans six route files plus PlayAnswerPanel. GameHeader appears on index, mode, quick-length, team-setup, and PlayStackHeader. AuthCard serves auth screens. HeaderBackButton is consumed through GameHeader slots.

PublicAuthEntry is consumed by LegalScrollScreen. Root, app, auth, and play routes hide headers and apply palette backgrounds. Admin routes also hide headers and redirect web traffic on native. RulesModal is implemented as a card stack rather than a modal presentation.

Admin routes are thin wrappers over the admin group. They re-export screens with AdminAccessBoundary instead of duplicating them. The docs/modals.md file describes Expo Router modal routes that the app does not actually use. The app relies on imperative React Native Modals or fixed overlays. Four modal patterns exist: WebAwareModal, WagerInfoModal, TopicColumnPickerModal, admin mobile sidebar Modal, and PromoModeDropdown Modal. Some screens re-implement scaffold components.

text:
Writing the audit artifact with findings.

Audit complete. Full write-up: `.pi-subagents/artifacts/outputs/43f2c386/.pi-subagents/artifacts/outputs/audit-primitives.md`

## Highlights

**Primitives:** `ScreenContent` is the thinnest shared wrapper (5 direct screens + `LegalScrollScreen` + `PlayScaffold`). Domain scaffolds are `PlayScaffold` (play flow), `LegalScrollScreen` (terms/privacy), `AuthCard` (auth), and `AdminShell`. `BootScreen`, `HeroSection`, and `ProfileAuthGate` have **zero** app imports.

**Layouts:** All stacks use `headerShown: false`. `(app)`, `(auth)`, and `play` share `landscapeStackScreenOptions` (`presentation: 'card'`). Only root `_layout` sets `contentStyle.backgroundColor` from the theme palette.

**Admin:** `app/admin/*` re-exports `app/(admin)/*` with `AdminAccessBoundary` — not duplicate implementations. Styles differ (admin chrome vs hub `ScreenContent`).

**Modals:** `docs/modals.md` describes Expo Router modal routes; the app uses **no** `presentation: 'modal'`. Overlays use RN `Modal` or web `position: fixed` via `WebAwareModal` (settings only) and duplicated patterns in play modals.

**Gap:** Most screens re-implement safe area + scroll + gutter. `ScreenContent` is always `fullWidth`, so `LAYOUT.contentMaxWidth` (560) is unused on hub screens.

**Recommendation:** Extend `ScreenContent` into a `Screen` shell owning safe area, background, gutter, optional scroll, and maxWidth. Keep `PlayScaffold` and `AdminShell` domain-specific. Consolidate play modals onto `WebAwareModal`.

```acceptance-report
{
  "criteriaSatisfied": [
    {
      "id": "criterion-1",
      "status": "satisfied",
      "evidence": "Full audit written to audit-primitives.md with paths, usage counts, severity-rated gaps, and Screen scaffold recommendation"
    }
  ],
  "changedFiles": [
    ".pi-subagents/artifacts/outputs/43f2c386/.pi-subagents/artifacts/outputs/audit-primitives.md"
  ],
  "testsAddedOrUpdated": [],
  "commandsRun": [
    {
      "command": "grep/glob/read (read-only audit)",
      "result": "passed",
      "summary": "Mapped primitives, layouts, admin routes, modals, and screen duplication patterns"
    }
  ],
  "validationOutput": [
    "ScreenContent: 5 direct + LegalScrollScreen + PlayScaffold",
    "WebAwareModal: settings only (5 instances)",
    "No presentation:'modal' in app code",
    "app/admin re-exports (admin) — not copies"
  ],
  "residualRisks": [
    "Per-screen table is pattern-based from grep + spot reads, not every file opened line-by-line"
  ],
  "noStagedFiles": true,
  "diffSummary": "Scout artifact only; no application code edited",
  "reviewFindings": [
    "high: ~15 screens duplicate safe-area + scroll + gutter",
    "medium: ScreenContent fullWidth bypasses contentMaxWidth",
    "medium: web modal pattern triplicated",
    "low: BootScreen/HeroSection/ProfileAuthGate unused"
  ],
  "manualNotes": "Start implementation at components/ScreenContent.tsx"
}
```
